ownerUpdates.text:
7 signs your mouth needs attention — even if nothing hurts. 🦷

Most dental problems don't announce themselves with pain. By the time it hurts, the problem is usually advanced.
Watch out for these:
1️⃣ Gums that bleed when brushing — not normal, early gum disease
2️⃣ Persistent bad breath despite brushing
3️⃣ Teeth that look shorter or flatter than before (grinding)
4️⃣ Sensitivity to hot, cold, or sweet that's getting worse
5️⃣ White spots or dark areas on teeth — early decay
6️⃣ Jaw clicking, popping, or morning stiffness
7️⃣ You haven't visited a dentist in over a year
Any one of these caught early = simple, affordable treatment.
Left too long = root canals, extractions, implants.
If any of these sound familiar, your mouth is asking for help.
📍 Damansara Perdana, Metropolitan Square PJ
📞 +6012-936 5116 (WhatsApp)
🕙 10am–10pm | Mon–Sat

What Should You Look for When Choosing a Dentist? 

Choosing the right dentist is about more than just convenience, it’s about finding a clinic you can trust with your oral health and confidence.

Here are a few important things to consider:
✔ Positive patient reviews and treatment results
✔ Experienced and qualified dental professionals
✔ Clear communication and transparent pricing
✔ Modern dental technology and equipment
✔ A comfortable, welcoming environment
✔ A wide range of services, including cosmetic and preventive care

A good dental experience should help patients feel informed, comfortable, and confident throughout their treatment journey.

At Damansara Dental Studio, we focus on personalized care, modern dentistry, and helping patients achieve healthy, confident smiles.

📩 Contact us today to book your consultation.

Nervous about wisdom tooth removal? Here's what to expect. 🦷
Wisdom tooth removal is one of the most common procedures we do at Damansara Dental Studio — and most patients tell us it was much easier than they expected.
The procedure:
You'll be under local anaesthesia the whole time, so you feel pressure but no pain. A simple extraction takes 15–20 minutes. Surgical cases (impacted teeth) take a little longer but are just as manageable.
Recovery tips:
✅ No straws for 5 days (prevents dry socket)
✅ Sleep with head elevated for 2 nights
✅ Soft foods only — yes, ice cream counts
✅ Rinse with warm salt water after meals
✅ Take the full course of antibiotics if prescribed
Most patients feel back to normal within 7 days.
If you have a wisdom tooth that's been bothering you, don't wait until it becomes an emergency. Early removal means easier recovery.
📍 Damansara Perdana, Metropolitan Square PJ
📞 +6012-936 5116 (WhatsApp)
🕙 Open 10am–10pm, Mon–Sat

Invisalign vs Braces in Malaysia: Which Is Right for You?

Braces or Invisalign? It's the question every Malaysian orthodontic patient faces. Both deliver results — but the experience is worlds apart.

How they work
Braces use metal or ceramic brackets bonded to your teeth, tightened by your orthodontist over time. They're fixed — nothing for you to manage. Invisalign uses clear removable trays you swap every one to two weeks. You're in control, which is both the appeal and the responsibility.

Lifestyle fit
Braces restrict what you eat and require extra care when brushing, but once they're on, they just work. Invisalign is more comfortable and nearly invisible, but you need to wear the aligners 20–22 hours daily without fail. Forget that, and treatment slows down.

Timeline
Both options typically take 12 to 24 months. The difference is consistency — braces work around the clock automatically, while Invisalign results depend on how disciplined you are.

Which case suits which?
Invisalign handles mild-to-moderate cases well. For complex bite issues or severe crowding, braces still have the edge. Your orthodontist's recommendation matters most here — follow it.

The best treatment isn't the one that looks the coolest. It's the one that fits your teeth, your habits, and your life.

2. Nourishment: Growth, Healing, and Sustenance

The mouth is the starting point of nourishment, playing a vital role in overall health. Every nutrient begins its journey here, where food is broken down and prepared for digestion.

Teeth mechanically break food into smaller particles, while saliva initiates chemical digestion and aids swallowing. Taste receptors also influence dietary choices, shaping how the body responds to what we consume.

When oral function is compromised—due to missing teeth, gum disease, or discomfort—chewing becomes less effective, which can impact digestion, nutrient absorption, and overall wellbeing.

Beyond physical nutrition, eating is tied to comfort, culture, and emotional health. The mouth allows us to enjoy food and connect through shared experiences.

Maintaining good oral health supports efficient nourishment, helping the body function, heal, and thrive.

1. Breathing: The Beginning of Life. 

Breath is the foundation of life, and the oral cavity plays a more important role than most realise. While nasal breathing is ideal, the mouth acts as a vital backup airway, ensuring oxygen continues to reach the body when needed.

Oxygen fuels every cell, supporting energy, focus, and overall health. When breathing becomes inefficient, it can affect sleep quality, energy levels, and long-term wellbeing.

The health and structure of the oral cavity—jaw position, teeth alignment, and tongue posture—directly influence airflow. Issues like mouth breathing, restricted airways, or poor oral posture can lead to snoring, fatigue, and reduced oxygen intake.

Breathing is also linked to emotional balance. Calm, controlled breathing supports relaxation, while irregular patterns may reflect stress.

Understanding this connection shifts dentistry beyond teeth and gums. A healthy oral environment supports better breathing, improved oxygenation, and overall quality of life.

Why Oral Health Is More Important Than You Think

When people think about oral health, they often think about cavities or keeping their teeth white. But oral health goes far beyond appearance. It plays a major role in your overall health, energy levels, and even how your body functions daily.

Your mouth is full of bacteria, both good and bad. When oral hygiene is not maintained, harmful bacteria can grow and lead to problems like gum disease and tooth decay. These bacteria do not just stay in your mouth. They can enter your bloodstream and affect other parts of your body.

Studies have shown links between poor oral health and conditions such as heart disease, diabetes, and respiratory issues. This means that taking care of your teeth and gums is not just about your smile. It is about protecting your entire body.

Simple habits like brushing, flossing, and regular dental check-ups can make a big difference. Prevention is always easier and more affordable than treatment.

Your mouth is the starting point of your health. When you take care of it, everything else benefits.

Bleeding Gums Are Not Normal: What Your Body Is Telling You

Many people ignore bleeding gums, thinking it is normal, especially when brushing or flossing. The truth is, healthy gums do not bleed. Bleeding is often one of the earliest signs of gum disease.

Gum disease begins with inflammation caused by plaque buildup. If left untreated, it can progress into more serious conditions that damage the gums and supporting bone. Over time, this can lead to tooth loss.

But the effects are not just limited to the mouth. Gum inflammation has been linked to systemic inflammation in the body, which can increase the risk of other health problems.

Bleeding gums are your body’s way of telling you that something is wrong. It is an early warning sign that should not be ignored.

The good news is that gum disease is preventable and treatable, especially when caught early. A professional cleaning, combined with good oral hygiene, can restore your gum health.

If you notice bleeding, it is time to take action, not wait.

The Hidden Connection Between Oral Health and Confidence

Your smile is one of the first things people notice about you. It plays a big role in how you present yourself and how others perceive you. But confidence is not just about having straight or white teeth. It starts with feeling comfortable and healthy in your own mouth.

When there are issues like bad breath, gum problems, or discomfort, it can affect how you speak, smile, and interact with others. Many people become more reserved without even realizing it.

On the other hand, good oral health allows you to express yourself freely. You smile more, speak with ease, and feel more confident in social and professional situations.

This is why dental care is not just about fixing problems. It is about improving quality of life. Small treatments and regular maintenance can have a big impact on how you feel every day.

Confidence starts from within, but your smile plays a powerful role in showing it to the world.

How Regular Dental Visits Protect More Than Just Your Teeth

Many people only visit the dentist when they feel pain. By then, the problem is often more advanced and requires more complex treatment.

Regular dental check-ups are designed to prevent problems before they start. During these visits, dentists check not only your teeth but also your gums, soft tissues, and overall oral condition.

Early signs of issues such as cavities, gum disease, or even systemic conditions can be detected during a routine examination. This allows for early treatment, which is usually simpler, less invasive, and more cost-effective.

Professional cleaning also removes plaque and tartar that cannot be removed by brushing alone. This helps maintain healthy gums and reduces the risk of disease.

Think of dental visits as part of your overall health routine, just like regular medical check-ups. They are not just about fixing problems. They are about preventing them.

A small commitment today can save you from bigger issues in the future.

reviews.name: Nelson Ng
reviews.text:
AVOID THIS PLACE AT ALL COSTS

We went in for emergency crown replacement. The only place opened at night so we went there. Instead of doing the crown replacement, she talked for 1 hour about the different treatments she can do for us and charged us RM150 consultation. Dr Lavina is a manipulative seller. She will plant these treatments into your head and make it seem like you THINK you need to do them. She said that she wants us to the treatment as in her own consciousness, she does not feel like doing an emergency crown replacement is the “right” thing to do.

Do not go here if you want to be scammed.
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Nelson,

Thank you for your feedback.

We respectfully disagree with the characterisation of the consultation described in your review and believe it is important to provide additional context for prospective patients reading this.

The consultation in question involved a dislodged crown. However, upon clinical examination and investigation, it became apparent that the situation extended beyond a simple crown re-cementation. Significant underlying concerns were identified, including signs of infection associated with a previously treated tooth. In such circumstances, simply re-attaching an existing crown without first addressing the underlying condition would not have been responsible treatment and could have compromised the patient's long-term oral health.

As dental professionals, our responsibility is not merely to provide the treatment requested, but to first establish an accurate diagnosis and recommend what is clinically appropriate. This sometimes means having difficult conversations when the most suitable treatment differs from the quick solution initially anticipated.

The time spent during the consultation was dedicated to examination, diagnosis, review of findings, discussion of the prognosis of the affected tooth, and explanation of the available treatment options. This was done to ensure that the patient and accompanying family members had sufficient information to make an informed decision regarding future care.

The fee charged reflects registration, professional assessment, clinical expertise, and time invested in evaluating the condition and explaining the available options. No patient is under any obligation to proceed with recommended treatment.

We categorically reject the allegation that our clinic engages in scams or manipulative sales practices. In fact, the procedure requested was not carried out precisely because we were unwilling to provide what we believed to be an inappropriate short-term solution without first addressing the underlying dental condition.

Our philosophy has always been to place patient welfare above convenience. We would rather spend time ensuring a patient fully understands their diagnosis and treatment options than provide a temporary fix that fails to address the root cause of the problem.

We remain committed to ethical, evidence-based dentistry and stand by the professional judgment exercised during that consultation.

We wish you and your family well.

reviews.text:
If you're considering braces, Damansara Dental Studio, is absolutely wonderful from start to finish.

I had my braces done here and honestly, the entire 1 year journey was nothing short of amazing. Before coming to DDS, I had enquired at several other clinics and was told it would take at least 3 years. Dr. Lavina and the team somehow made it happen in just 1 year, I still can't believe it! The results speak for themselves and I couldn't be happier with my smile.

What truly sets this clinic apart is how warm and welcoming everyone is the moment you walk through the door. From the very first consultation, I felt completely at ease, like I was being taken care of by family. The team never made me feel rushed or anxious. They walked me through every single step of the process, explaining what they were doing and what to expect next, which made such a huge difference.

Throughout the entire year, every appointment was smooth, comfortable, and thorough. Dr. Lavina's attention to detail and genuine care for her patients is something I have never experienced at any other clinic. The small gestures, the reassuring words, the patience in answering every question I had, the warm smiles made every visit something I actually looked forward to.

The clinic itself is clean, modern, and has such a calming atmosphere. You never feel like you're just another patient here. You feel seen, heard, and genuinely cared for.

Thank you so much Dr. Lavina and the entire DDS team for this incredible journey. You gave me more than just a beautiful smile, you gave me confidence. Truly the best dental experience I have ever had! 🥰🌹

reviews.name: Ema Sense
reviews.text:
Overall, the experience was good. Dr. Choo is very patient and takes the time to explain things in detail.

Follow up review:
I am updating my previous review because my experience with the payment process and customer service has been consistently disappointing.

Previously, I was encouraged to leave a review in exchange for an RM50 voucher that could be used on a future payment. I left the review and received the voucher. However, during my visit today, after making payment, I presented the voucher only a few seconds after tapping my card. The staff refused to honor it and told me to use it on my next visit instead.

The reason given was that the payment had already been processed. While I understand there may be procedures in place, the delay was literally a matter of seconds. Considering that I have spent thousands of ringgit on treatments here, I found the response unnecessarily rigid and lacking any effort to accommodate a loyal customer. There were many possible solutions, such as reversing the transaction or issuing a refund adjustment, but none were even considered.

What disappointed me most is that this is not the first time I have encountered something similar.

On a previous occasion, I came for a consultation and later decided to proceed with treatment. During my first treatment appointment, I noticed a promotion for new customers displayed at the counter. I had never been informed about it. When I enquired, I was told that I was not eligible because I had visited before. However, my earlier visit was merely a free consultation—I had not yet engaged any paid services. Despite this, the promotion was denied and I was charged the full amount.

At the time, I let it go. However, after experiencing another rigid rejection today, I felt compelled to share my feedback. The issue is not the RM50. The issue is the attitude towards customers. When a customer has spent a significant amount of money with a clinic, a few seconds' delay in presenting a voucher should not be treated as a reason to deny it outright. Good customer service requires discretion, flexibility, and a willingness to help, not simply hiding behind procedures.

To be clear, my previous positive review of the dentist still stands. The doctor has been professional and competent throughout the treatment. Unfortunately, the same cannot be said for the clinic's payment process and customer service. For a clinic that charges these prices, I expected a much higher standard of customer care and flexibility. Instead, I left feeling that customer loyalty is taken for granted and that providing a positive customer experience is not a priority.

reviews.name: LAI AMI
reviews.text:
真心觉得踩雷牙科诊所！
事情是这样的。我的智齿发炎了就预约了Dr Amy 。她就开了抗生素给我服用，等到发炎好了就预约要拔掉智齿。拍了x-ray,也买了各种药物，漱口液等等。。Dr Chu 一直吓我说我的智齿很难拔！需要正式的开刀牙医来上门帮我拔。她们不会这个专业！(开牙科诊所居然也有不会拔的牙）我问了价钱之后要RM2,500一颗！Reception Ann一直跟我说如果我去外面牙科诊所拔的话会损伤我的神经线，搞不好会脸瘫痪之类的！
结果我去了朋友介绍的另一家牙科诊所只需要10分钟就把我的智慧牙给拔了。。而且价钱只需要这里的5分之一！真心不推荐这里！都只是要赚钱而已，并不是真心服务的！
reviews.textTranslated:
I honestly feel like I made a terrible mistake at this dental clinic!

Here's what happened. My wisdom tooth got inflamed, so I made an appointment with Dr. Amy. She prescribed antibiotics, and once the inflammation subsided, I scheduled an appointment to have it extracted. I had an X-ray taken and bought various medications, mouthwash, etc. Dr. Chu kept scaring me, saying my wisdom tooth was difficult to extract and required a professional surgeon to come to my home. She claimed they weren't qualified! (How could a dental clinic not know how to extract teeth?) I asked about the price, and it was RM2,500 per tooth! Receptionist Ann kept telling me that if I went to an outside clinic, it could damage my nerves and potentially cause facial paralysis!

In the end, I went to another dental clinic recommended by a friend, and they extracted my wisdom tooth in just 10 minutes... and at only one-fifth the price! I sincerely don't recommend this clinic! They're just after money; they don't genuinely care about service!
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Hi ms lai, we have received your feedback on the clinic and 
we want to express our regret for any distress or inconvenience you encountered during your visits to our clinic.

It's disheartening to hear that your initial interactions with us left you feeling uneasy.

We apologize for any misinformation or concerns raised about the extraction process. We understand the the charges of extraction by specialist oral surgeon might be on the higher side and thus we suggested the surgical removal of the wisdom tooth to be done by our regular dentist where the cost of removal is around rm850-1200 and initially you are agreeable to it. 

As for the receptionist, we believe that there maybe some misunderstandings in explanations as we never discouraged patients from seeking second opinions and  treatments from other clinics. Patients are always free to get treatment wherever they prefer to. However, what we are trying to inform was due to the very close proximity of the nerve to the root of the tooth, there may be a higher possibility of nerve injury regardless of where the extraction being done and we are actually trying to be extra cautious. 

Regarding the cost concerns you've raised, we understand the financial aspect of healthcare is crucial. We are actively reviewing our pricing structure to ensure it remains fair and competitive within the industry. We genuinely appreciate your honesty in this matter.

We're pleased to hear that you found a satisfactory solution at another dental clinic. While we regret that it wasn't with us, we genuinely wish you a smooth recovery and continued oral health.


Thank you and kind regards.

ownerUpdates.text:
😬 自己的牙齿看起来黄黄的，别人的都白白的？
每天都刷牙，也买了美白牙膏，还是没用。

🔬 牙齿从里面变黄的3个原因 :

✅药物影响
8岁以下吃过 Tetracycline（一种抗生素），或者妈妈怀孕时吃过，这种药会跟还在发育中的牙齿里的钙结合在一起。结果就是——灰色、棕色或黄色的纹路，永远都不会消失。

✅氟斑牙
牙齿还在牙龈里发育的时候，接触到太多氟化物。会造成白色或棕色的斑点。

✅外伤
小时候撞到牙齿，可能伤到里面的神经——牙齿慢慢变灰，即使一开始看起来完全没事。

❓ 为什么药物💊会让牙齿变黄？
Tetracycline 对钙离子有很强的吸附力。一旦进入身体，它就会到处去找钙结合——包括还在发育中的牙齿。
它是直接嵌进牙齿结构里面的。不是表面的污渍，而是已经变成牙齿的一部分了。
最高风险的时期：怀孕中期一直到孩子12岁。

👨🏻‍⚕️ 今天正常营业 9am-9pm🌙 😊

Dental Clinic open as usual today

🎉 终于拆牙箍了，开心还没多久，结果发现牙齿上有白色斑点？

"拆完牙箍变成这样，正常吗🤔？"

🔬 这到底是怎么回事
那些白色斑点叫做白斑病变(white spot lesion)，意思是牙釉质因为矿物质流失而开始有早期损伤了。

戴牙箍的时候，bracket 周围的位置很难刷干净。牙菌膜慢慢堆积，细菌产生酸，酸把牙釉质里的矿物质溶解掉。结果就变成那种粉笔状、暗暗的白色斑点。

😟 两个不同阶段
第一阶段——牙釉质矿物质流失，但还没变成蛀牙。还可以治疗补救。

第二阶段——已经形成早期蛀洞了。表面变粗糙，有小小的孔洞。需要更密集的治疗。

🛠️ 三个治疗方案
✅ 氟化物涂层或者 CPP-ACP——帮助矿物质重新沉积回牙釉质里。适合早期的情况。
✅ 微研磨——轻轻磨掉一层薄薄的表面，减少斑点的明显程度。适合中等程度的情况。
✅ 树脂渗透——让树脂渗入牙釉质，把斑点遮盖掉，不需要磨掉表层。对情况已经稳定的病例来说是最好的选择。

🔬 2026年马来西亚私人诊所4种牙冠类型和价格

金属牙冠 — RM700 至 RM1,300
四种里面最强的，可以用 20 到 30 年。颜色是灰色金属色——最适合最里面的大臼齿，笑起来完全看不到的那种。

PFM牙冠 — RM900 至 RM1,500
外面是白色瓷，里面有金属框架。时间久了，牙龈往下退的时候牙龈边缘会开始出现一条黑线。以前很流行——现在已经慢慢被取代了。

氧化锆牙冠 — RM1,200 至 RM2,500
目前马来西亚最受欢迎的选择。完全没有金属，够强，颜色自然。2025年的研究显示5年存活率高达98%。前牙后牙都适合。

全瓷牙冠 — RM1,500 至 RM3,000
外观最自然最好看。最适合笑起来会露出来的前牙。承受咬合压力方面比氧化锆稍弱，不太适合后牙。

🏆 2026年最受欢迎的选择
氧化锆完全主导市场——全球81%的牙冠现在都用氧化锆做，PFM只剩7%了。马来西亚也是同样的趋势。

为什么？够强、好看、没有金属。

❓假牙、牙冠、还是种植牙——哪个咬苹果🍎最有力？🤔

这三种的咬合力差别很大。这个差别直接决定了你做完治疗之后的生活质量。

🏆 种植牙咬合力最强 💪

假牙——只放在牙龈上面
没有牙根，没有连接到骨头。咬合力是最弱的😣——大概只有 50 牛顿左右。每次咬硬的东西，假牙都会稍微移动。

牙冠——套在原本的牙齿上面
咬合力取决于下面那颗牙的健康状况。如果牙齿或牙根已经弱了，或者骨头已经萎缩，咬合力也会跟着受限。

种植牙——直接把钛金属柱种进骨头里
用的是钛合金材料，会通过骨整合和下颌骨融为一体。咬合力直接传到骨头——可以达到将近 150 牛顿，非常接近天然牙齿的 200 牛顿。

😟 戴假牙的患者常常说：
"不敢吃肉，怕假牙移位。"

换成种植牙——牛排🍖、鱿鱼、苹果🍎——全部都可以吃。因为咬合力几乎和真牙一样。

🧠 医生通常没有解释清楚的事——这个咬合力的差距不只是舒不舒服的问题。它决定了你接下来是可以自由地想吃什么就吃什么，还是一辈子都要挑着吃。

🤔 知道不能，但为什么❓

背后的科学原理到底是什么
来，让医生解释一下

如果你真的了解拔牙后那个洞里发生了什么，你在头 48 小时内根本不会敢碰吸管

拔牙之后，血小板会在 15 到 30 分钟内在洞里形成血块

那个血块不只是普通的凝固血液

它是一个活的支架，是新组织生长和从里面慢慢封闭伤口的基础结构

当你用吸管吸东西，嘴巴里会产生负压

那个压力足够在一口吸的瞬间把血块直接拉出来

血块一旦脱落，骨头和神经就直接暴露在空气和细菌面前

这个情况叫做干槽症

痛的来源是暴露在外的骨头神经，不是普通的伤口痛

即使吃了止痛药，也可能持续痛 3 到 5 天

很多人不知道的是——干槽症不会自己好

必须回诊所让医生清洁伤口并放入特殊敷料，愈合过程才能重新开始

医生通常没解释清楚的事——用吸管看起来只是轻轻一吸，但产生的负压原理跟真空吸力是一样的

拔牙后头 48 小时，那个洞根本没有足够的力量抵抗这个压力

.

拔牙后的「四个NO🚫」
很多人以为没关系，结果痛到后悔 😔

❌ 第一个NO：不要能用吸管

很多人以为用吸管喝东西比较安全，因为不用直接碰到伤口。
其实刚好相反。
用吸管吸的那一刻，嘴巴里产生的负压会直接把血块吸走。
血块一旦脱落，骨头和神经就直接暴露了。
不管喝什么——水、果汁、奶茶——拔牙后 72 小时内，放下吸管。

---

❌ 第二个NO：不要漱口

拔完牙当天很多人第一个反应就是想漱口，感觉嘴巴里有血腥味很不舒服。
但用力漱口产生的水流冲击力，足够把还没稳固的血块直接冲走。
如果真的很想清洁口腔，可以轻轻用盐水含着然后让它自己流出来，不要用力漱。
漱口水、Listerine 也先暂时放一边，至少等 24 小时后再说。

---

❌ 第三个NO：不要抽烟

这个是干槽症最常见的原因之一。
抽烟有双重伤害——
第一，吸烟的动作跟用吸管一样，嘴巴产生负压，血块容易脱落。
第二，烟里面的化学物质会干扰血液凝固，让伤口更难愈合。
研究显示，抽烟的人得干槽症的概率比不抽烟的人高出将近三倍。
拔牙后至少 72 小时，能忍就忍。

---

❌ 第四个NO：不要用舌头去戳那个洞

说真的，这个几乎每个人都会做 😅
洞在那里，舌头自然就想去探一下，感觉很奇怪很想确认一下。
但每次舌头碰到那个洞，都是在干扰血块形成的过程。
血块很脆弱，尤其是头 24 到 48 小时。
一直戳，一直干扰，愈合就一直被推迟。
脑袋知道不该碰，但舌头不听话——这个真的要靠意识去控制。

---

⚠️ 总结一句话

拔牙后的痛不痛，很大程度上不是看医生技术好不好，而是看你回家之后有没有好好保护那个血块。

血块在，愈合顺利。
血块没了，干槽症来了，那种痛才是真的让你后悔 😔

🦷
Our Dental clinic in Petaling Jaya open as usual today (Agong's Birthday) to serve the community of Seapark Taman Paramount 🏡.

❓上一次拔牙完全不痛，麻药退了之后最多吃两颗止痛药就没事了

但是……
第二次找同一个医生拔牙
这次却痛到晚上还在跳

这个医生是不是退步了😣？

其实答案在你自己身体里，

四个4️⃣ 决定拔牙后疼痛程度的原因：

1) 拔牙时牙齿的状况
有活跃性感染的牙齿，拔完之后会比较痛
感染会改变周围组织的酸碱值，让麻药更难完全封住神经，发炎的组织在愈合过程中也会更敏感

2) 拔牙的难度
拔一颗已经松动的牙跟拔一颗牙根分叉或者在骨头里转弯的牙完全不一样
处理得越久，组织受到创伤的时间就越长

3) Dry socket（干槽症）
本来应该盖住拔牙洞的血块提早脱落了
骨头直接暴露在外，神经被空气和压力直接刺激
所以有时候第二天或第三天反而比第一天更痛

4) 你拔完之后做了什么（上次不痛，这次会不会就没那么认真跟着医生的指示做？）
用吸管喝东西、用力漱口、吃热的东西、抽烟
这些都会把血块冲掉，让已经开始愈合的地方重新暴露

🦷

reviews.name: Meryem Kuzu
reviews.text:
I visited this dental clinic for the first time after not having a check-up in about two years here in Malaysia. It was recommended by a colleague and conveniently located near me. I had been dealing with a toothache and gum inflammation for the past three days, which even triggered a migraine, so I was honestly a bit anxious going in.

Dr. Ju Lyn was excellent throughout the entire visit. She took the time to clearly explain my tooth condition, walked me through the X-ray results, and outlined what I should address and other suggestions. I really appreciated how thorough and reassuring she was.

I ended up getting a general check-up, X-ray, and filling done, and the whole process was smooth and completely painless. She was very knowledgeable, attentive, and made me feel at ease the entire time.

Overall, a very positive experience from booking to the overall procedure - I’ll definitely be coming back.
reviews.responseFromOwnerText: It was our pleasure having you over today. We are glad that you had. A good experience. Thank you for your feedback. We hope that your dental issues are addressed. See you again!

reviews.name: Kit Shaun
reviews.text:
Great experience at this clinic, so much so that I genuinely felt compelled to share it. From the moment I walked in, Prema at the front desk set the tone, being incredibly warm, friendly, and welcoming. It’s rare to feel so at ease in a dental setting, but she made the entire registration process smooth and pleasant.

Dr Julynn was exceptional. She was detailed, patient, and meticulous every step of the way, from taking the time to explain everything for my layman brain to understand, to making sure I was comfortable throughout. What stood out the most was how thorough she was, you can tell she genuinely cares about her patients and takes pride in her work. I never felt rushed, and every concern I had was addressed.

Overall, this clinic exceeded my expectations in every way. Professional, caring, and genuinely dedicated to patient comfort, I couldn’t recommend them more highly!

I brought my daughter 5-year-old to Oralis Dental after she suffered a broken tooth and bleeding from a fall. She was very scared and anxious at first, but the dentist was incredibly patient and knew exactly how to comfort and reassure her throughout the examination.

The dentist was kind, gentle, and made my doughter feel comfortable and safe during the visit. They also took the time to explain the condition and treatment options clearly, which I greatly appreciated.

The clinic and treatment room were clean, comfortable, and welcoming. We are very satisfied with the care and service provided. Thank you to the dentist and the entire team at Oralis Dental for such a positive experience. Highly recommended! ❤️🦷✨
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Amiey Hasri,

Thank you so much for your heartfelt 5-star review! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

We're truly honored that you chose Oralis Dental during what must have been a stressful and worrying time for both you and your daughter. Seeing a child experience a dental injury can be frightening, and we're glad our team was able to provide comfort and reassurance throughout her visit.

It's wonderful to hear that the dentist's gentle and patient approach helped your daughter feel safe and at ease despite her initial fear and anxiety. Creating positive dental experiences for children, especially during emergency situations, is something we deeply care about.

We're also pleased that you found our explanations clear and helpful, and that our clinic environment made both of you feel comfortable and welcomed. Providing compassionate care while ensuring parents fully understand their child's condition and treatment options is always our priority.

Thank you for your trust, kind words, and recommendation. We wish your daughter a smooth recovery and look forward to caring for her smile for many years to come. ❤️🦷✨

Warm regards,
The Oralis Dental Team

Dr. Ting Ann Nee was an excellent dentist to me. She has been my dentist since 2016 from Damon braces to now root canal.

I kept returning to her even though I lived further away because she was trustworthy, patience, understanding and very kind. She always gave good professional advice, not forcing to earn extra but really care for us and advice based on our perspective to let us decide freely.

She was testing and polishing back and forth then readjust, ensuring the perfect ceramic fit plus looking balance just so we can have a great looking smile.

In addition, Ting Dental is always upgrading their technology and service, which showed that they are eager to stay up to date and providing better and more comfortable dental service to their customers.

I highly recommended to visit them if you need a dental service, adults or kids, they'll take good care of us without breaking the wallet.

ownerUpdates.text:
😬 自己的牙齿看起来黄黄的，别人的都白白的？
每天都刷牙，也买了美白牙膏，还是没用。

🔬 牙齿从里面变黄的3个原因 :

✅药物影响
8岁以下吃过 Tetracycline（一种抗生素），或者妈妈怀孕时吃过，这种药会跟还在发育中的牙齿里的钙结合在一起。结果就是——灰色、棕色或黄色的纹路，永远都不会消失。

✅氟斑牙
牙齿还在牙龈里发育的时候，接触到太多氟化物。会造成白色或棕色的斑点。

✅外伤
小时候撞到牙齿，可能伤到里面的神经——牙齿慢慢变灰，即使一开始看起来完全没事。

❓ 为什么药物💊会让牙齿变黄？
Tetracycline 对钙离子有很强的吸附力。一旦进入身体，它就会到处去找钙结合——包括还在发育中的牙齿。
它是直接嵌进牙齿结构里面的。不是表面的污渍，而是已经变成牙齿的一部分了。
最高风险的时期：怀孕中期一直到孩子12岁。

👨🏻‍⚕️ 今天正常营业 9am-9pm🌙 😊

Dental Clinic open as usual today

🎉 终于拆牙箍了，开心还没多久，结果发现牙齿上有白色斑点？

"拆完牙箍变成这样，正常吗🤔？"

🔬 这到底是怎么回事
那些白色斑点叫做白斑病变(white spot lesion)，意思是牙釉质因为矿物质流失而开始有早期损伤了。

戴牙箍的时候，bracket 周围的位置很难刷干净。牙菌膜慢慢堆积，细菌产生酸，酸把牙釉质里的矿物质溶解掉。结果就变成那种粉笔状、暗暗的白色斑点。

😟 两个不同阶段
第一阶段——牙釉质矿物质流失，但还没变成蛀牙。还可以治疗补救。

第二阶段——已经形成早期蛀洞了。表面变粗糙，有小小的孔洞。需要更密集的治疗。

🛠️ 三个治疗方案
✅ 氟化物涂层或者 CPP-ACP——帮助矿物质重新沉积回牙釉质里。适合早期的情况。
✅ 微研磨——轻轻磨掉一层薄薄的表面，减少斑点的明显程度。适合中等程度的情况。
✅ 树脂渗透——让树脂渗入牙釉质，把斑点遮盖掉，不需要磨掉表层。对情况已经稳定的病例来说是最好的选择。

🔬 2026年马来西亚私人诊所4种牙冠类型和价格

金属牙冠 — RM700 至 RM1,300
四种里面最强的，可以用 20 到 30 年。颜色是灰色金属色——最适合最里面的大臼齿，笑起来完全看不到的那种。

PFM牙冠 — RM900 至 RM1,500
外面是白色瓷，里面有金属框架。时间久了，牙龈往下退的时候牙龈边缘会开始出现一条黑线。以前很流行——现在已经慢慢被取代了。

氧化锆牙冠 — RM1,200 至 RM2,500
目前马来西亚最受欢迎的选择。完全没有金属，够强，颜色自然。2025年的研究显示5年存活率高达98%。前牙后牙都适合。

全瓷牙冠 — RM1,500 至 RM3,000
外观最自然最好看。最适合笑起来会露出来的前牙。承受咬合压力方面比氧化锆稍弱，不太适合后牙。

🏆 2026年最受欢迎的选择
氧化锆完全主导市场——全球81%的牙冠现在都用氧化锆做，PFM只剩7%了。马来西亚也是同样的趋势。

为什么？够强、好看、没有金属。

❓假牙、牙冠、还是种植牙——哪个咬苹果🍎最有力？🤔

这三种的咬合力差别很大。这个差别直接决定了你做完治疗之后的生活质量。

🏆 种植牙咬合力最强 💪

假牙——只放在牙龈上面
没有牙根，没有连接到骨头。咬合力是最弱的😣——大概只有 50 牛顿左右。每次咬硬的东西，假牙都会稍微移动。

牙冠——套在原本的牙齿上面
咬合力取决于下面那颗牙的健康状况。如果牙齿或牙根已经弱了，或者骨头已经萎缩，咬合力也会跟着受限。

种植牙——直接把钛金属柱种进骨头里
用的是钛合金材料，会通过骨整合和下颌骨融为一体。咬合力直接传到骨头——可以达到将近 150 牛顿，非常接近天然牙齿的 200 牛顿。

😟 戴假牙的患者常常说：
"不敢吃肉，怕假牙移位。"

换成种植牙——牛排🍖、鱿鱼、苹果🍎——全部都可以吃。因为咬合力几乎和真牙一样。

🧠 医生通常没有解释清楚的事——这个咬合力的差距不只是舒不舒服的问题。它决定了你接下来是可以自由地想吃什么就吃什么，还是一辈子都要挑着吃。

🤔 知道不能，但为什么❓

背后的科学原理到底是什么
来，让医生解释一下

如果你真的了解拔牙后那个洞里发生了什么，你在头 48 小时内根本不会敢碰吸管

拔牙之后，血小板会在 15 到 30 分钟内在洞里形成血块

那个血块不只是普通的凝固血液

它是一个活的支架，是新组织生长和从里面慢慢封闭伤口的基础结构

当你用吸管吸东西，嘴巴里会产生负压

那个压力足够在一口吸的瞬间把血块直接拉出来

血块一旦脱落，骨头和神经就直接暴露在空气和细菌面前

这个情况叫做干槽症

痛的来源是暴露在外的骨头神经，不是普通的伤口痛

即使吃了止痛药，也可能持续痛 3 到 5 天

很多人不知道的是——干槽症不会自己好

必须回诊所让医生清洁伤口并放入特殊敷料，愈合过程才能重新开始

医生通常没解释清楚的事——用吸管看起来只是轻轻一吸，但产生的负压原理跟真空吸力是一样的

拔牙后头 48 小时，那个洞根本没有足够的力量抵抗这个压力

Yes😍 We are OPEN as usual today to serve the community 🏡 Dental Clinic still open at Night at Petaling Jaya.
Call us at 016-331 8772 
.

拔牙后的「四个NO🚫」
很多人以为没关系，结果痛到后悔 😔

❌ 第一个NO：不要能用吸管

很多人以为用吸管喝东西比较安全，因为不用直接碰到伤口。
其实刚好相反。
用吸管吸的那一刻，嘴巴里产生的负压会直接把血块吸走。
血块一旦脱落，骨头和神经就直接暴露了。
不管喝什么——水、果汁、奶茶——拔牙后 72 小时内，放下吸管。

---

❌ 第二个NO：不要漱口

拔完牙当天很多人第一个反应就是想漱口，感觉嘴巴里有血腥味很不舒服。
但用力漱口产生的水流冲击力，足够把还没稳固的血块直接冲走。
如果真的很想清洁口腔，可以轻轻用盐水含着然后让它自己流出来，不要用力漱。
漱口水、Listerine 也先暂时放一边，至少等 24 小时后再说。

---

❌ 第三个NO：不要抽烟

这个是干槽症最常见的原因之一。
抽烟有双重伤害——
第一，吸烟的动作跟用吸管一样，嘴巴产生负压，血块容易脱落。
第二，烟里面的化学物质会干扰血液凝固，让伤口更难愈合。
研究显示，抽烟的人得干槽症的概率比不抽烟的人高出将近三倍。
拔牙后至少 72 小时，能忍就忍。

---

❌ 第四个NO：不要用舌头去戳那个洞

说真的，这个几乎每个人都会做 😅
洞在那里，舌头自然就想去探一下，感觉很奇怪很想确认一下。
但每次舌头碰到那个洞，都是在干扰血块形成的过程。
血块很脆弱，尤其是头 24 到 48 小时。
一直戳，一直干扰，愈合就一直被推迟。
脑袋知道不该碰，但舌头不听话——这个真的要靠意识去控制。

---

⚠️ 总结一句话

拔牙后的痛不痛，很大程度上不是看医生技术好不好，而是看你回家之后有没有好好保护那个血块。

血块在，愈合顺利。
血块没了，干槽症来了，那种痛才是真的让你后悔 😔

🦷
Our Dental clinic in Petaling Jaya open as usual today (Agong's Birthday) to serve the community of Seapark Taman Paramount 🏡.

❓上一次拔牙完全不痛，麻药退了之后最多吃两颗止痛药就没事了

但是……
第二次找同一个医生拔牙
这次却痛到晚上还在跳

这个医生是不是退步了😣？

其实答案在你自己身体里，

四个4️⃣ 决定拔牙后疼痛程度的原因：

1) 拔牙时牙齿的状况
有活跃性感染的牙齿，拔完之后会比较痛
感染会改变周围组织的酸碱值，让麻药更难完全封住神经，发炎的组织在愈合过程中也会更敏感

2) 拔牙的难度
拔一颗已经松动的牙跟拔一颗牙根分叉或者在骨头里转弯的牙完全不一样
处理得越久，组织受到创伤的时间就越长

3) Dry socket（干槽症）
本来应该盖住拔牙洞的血块提早脱落了
骨头直接暴露在外，神经被空气和压力直接刺激
所以有时候第二天或第三天反而比第一天更痛

4) 你拔完之后做了什么（上次不痛，这次会不会就没那么认真跟着医生的指示做？）
用吸管喝东西、用力漱口、吃热的东西、抽烟
这些都会把血块冲掉，让已经开始愈合的地方重新暴露

🦷

每天都刷牙，也买了美白牙膏，还是没用。

✅药物影响
8岁以下吃过 Tetracycline（一种抗生素），或者妈妈怀孕时吃过，这种药会跟还在发育中的牙齿里的钙结合在一起。结果就是——灰色、棕色或黄色的纹路，永远都不会消失。

✅氟斑牙
牙齿还在牙龈里发育的时候，接触到太多氟化物。会造成白色或棕色的斑点。

✅外伤
小时候撞到牙齿，可能伤到里面的神经——牙齿慢慢变灰，即使一开始看起来完全没事。

❓ 为什么药物💊会让牙齿变黄？
Tetracycline 对钙离子有很强的吸附力。一旦进入身体，它就会到处去找钙结合——包括还在发育中的牙齿。
它是直接嵌进牙齿结构里面的。不是表面的污渍，而是已经变成牙齿的一部分了。
最高风险的时期：怀孕中期一直到孩子12岁。

👨🏻‍⚕️ 今天正常营业 9am-9pm🌙 😊

Dental Clinic open as usual today

🎉 终于拆牙箍了，开心还没多久，结果发现牙齿上有白色斑点？

"拆完牙箍变成这样，正常吗🤔？"

🔬 这到底是怎么回事
那些白色斑点叫做白斑病变(white spot lesion)，意思是牙釉质因为矿物质流失而开始有早期损伤了。

戴牙箍的时候，bracket 周围的位置很难刷干净。牙菌膜慢慢堆积，细菌产生酸，酸把牙釉质里的矿物质溶解掉。结果就变成那种粉笔状、暗暗的白色斑点。

😟 两个不同阶段
第一阶段——牙釉质矿物质流失，但还没变成蛀牙。还可以治疗补救。

第二阶段——已经形成早期蛀洞了。表面变粗糙，有小小的孔洞。需要更密集的治疗。

🛠️ 三个治疗方案
✅ 氟化物涂层或者 CPP-ACP——帮助矿物质重新沉积回牙釉质里。适合早期的情况。
✅ 微研磨——轻轻磨掉一层薄薄的表面，减少斑点的明显程度。适合中等程度的情况。
✅ 树脂渗透——让树脂渗入牙釉质，把斑点遮盖掉，不需要磨掉表层。对情况已经稳定的病例来说是最好的选择。

🔬 2026年马来西亚私人诊所4种牙冠类型和价格

金属牙冠 — RM700 至 RM1,300
四种里面最强的，可以用 20 到 30 年。颜色是灰色金属色——最适合最里面的大臼齿，笑起来完全看不到的那种。

PFM牙冠 — RM900 至 RM1,500
外面是白色瓷，里面有金属框架。时间久了，牙龈往下退的时候牙龈边缘会开始出现一条黑线。以前很流行——现在已经慢慢被取代了。

氧化锆牙冠 — RM1,200 至 RM2,500
目前马来西亚最受欢迎的选择。完全没有金属，够强，颜色自然。2025年的研究显示5年存活率高达98%。前牙后牙都适合。

全瓷牙冠 — RM1,500 至 RM3,000
外观最自然最好看。最适合笑起来会露出来的前牙。承受咬合压力方面比氧化锆稍弱，不太适合后牙。

🏆 2026年最受欢迎的选择
氧化锆完全主导市场——全球81%的牙冠现在都用氧化锆做，PFM只剩7%了。马来西亚也是同样的趋势。

为什么？够强、好看、没有金属。

❓假牙、牙冠、还是种植牙——哪个咬苹果🍎最有力？🤔

这三种的咬合力差别很大。这个差别直接决定了你做完治疗之后的生活质量。

🏆 种植牙咬合力最强 💪

假牙——只放在牙龈上面
没有牙根，没有连接到骨头。咬合力是最弱的😣——大概只有 50 牛顿左右。每次咬硬的东西，假牙都会稍微移动。

牙冠——套在原本的牙齿上面
咬合力取决于下面那颗牙的健康状况。如果牙齿或牙根已经弱了，或者骨头已经萎缩，咬合力也会跟着受限。

种植牙——直接把钛金属柱种进骨头里
用的是钛合金材料，会通过骨整合和下颌骨融为一体。咬合力直接传到骨头——可以达到将近 150 牛顿，非常接近天然牙齿的 200 牛顿。

😟 戴假牙的患者常常说：
"不敢吃肉，怕假牙移位。"

换成种植牙——牛排🍖、鱿鱼、苹果🍎——全部都可以吃。因为咬合力几乎和真牙一样。

🧠 医生通常没有解释清楚的事——这个咬合力的差距不只是舒不舒服的问题。它决定了你接下来是可以自由地想吃什么就吃什么，还是一辈子都要挑着吃。

🤔 知道不能，但为什么❓

背后的科学原理到底是什么
来，让医生解释一下

如果你真的了解拔牙后那个洞里发生了什么，你在头 48 小时内根本不会敢碰吸管

拔牙之后，血小板会在 15 到 30 分钟内在洞里形成血块

那个血块不只是普通的凝固血液

它是一个活的支架，是新组织生长和从里面慢慢封闭伤口的基础结构

当你用吸管吸东西，嘴巴里会产生负压

那个压力足够在一口吸的瞬间把血块直接拉出来

血块一旦脱落，骨头和神经就直接暴露在空气和细菌面前

这个情况叫做干槽症

痛的来源是暴露在外的骨头神经，不是普通的伤口痛

即使吃了止痛药，也可能持续痛 3 到 5 天

很多人不知道的是——干槽症不会自己好

必须回诊所让医生清洁伤口并放入特殊敷料，愈合过程才能重新开始

医生通常没解释清楚的事——用吸管看起来只是轻轻一吸，但产生的负压原理跟真空吸力是一样的

拔牙后头 48 小时，那个洞根本没有足够的力量抵抗这个压力

.

拔牙后的「四个NO🚫」
很多人以为没关系，结果痛到后悔 😔

❌ 第一个NO：不要能用吸管

很多人以为用吸管喝东西比较安全，因为不用直接碰到伤口。
其实刚好相反。
用吸管吸的那一刻，嘴巴里产生的负压会直接把血块吸走。
血块一旦脱落，骨头和神经就直接暴露了。
不管喝什么——水、果汁、奶茶——拔牙后 72 小时内，放下吸管。

---

❌ 第二个NO：不要漱口

拔完牙当天很多人第一个反应就是想漱口，感觉嘴巴里有血腥味很不舒服。
但用力漱口产生的水流冲击力，足够把还没稳固的血块直接冲走。
如果真的很想清洁口腔，可以轻轻用盐水含着然后让它自己流出来，不要用力漱。
漱口水、Listerine 也先暂时放一边，至少等 24 小时后再说。

---

❌ 第三个NO：不要抽烟

这个是干槽症最常见的原因之一。
抽烟有双重伤害——
第一，吸烟的动作跟用吸管一样，嘴巴产生负压，血块容易脱落。
第二，烟里面的化学物质会干扰血液凝固，让伤口更难愈合。
研究显示，抽烟的人得干槽症的概率比不抽烟的人高出将近三倍。
拔牙后至少 72 小时，能忍就忍。

---

❌ 第四个NO：不要用舌头去戳那个洞

说真的，这个几乎每个人都会做 😅
洞在那里，舌头自然就想去探一下，感觉很奇怪很想确认一下。
但每次舌头碰到那个洞，都是在干扰血块形成的过程。
血块很脆弱，尤其是头 24 到 48 小时。
一直戳，一直干扰，愈合就一直被推迟。
脑袋知道不该碰，但舌头不听话——这个真的要靠意识去控制。

---

⚠️ 总结一句话

拔牙后的痛不痛，很大程度上不是看医生技术好不好，而是看你回家之后有没有好好保护那个血块。

血块在，愈合顺利。
血块没了，干槽症来了，那种痛才是真的让你后悔 😔

🦷
Our Dental clinic in Petaling Jaya open as usual today (Agong's Birthday) to serve the community of Seapark Taman Paramount 🏡.

❓上一次拔牙完全不痛，麻药退了之后最多吃两颗止痛药就没事了

但是……
第二次找同一个医生拔牙
这次却痛到晚上还在跳

这个医生是不是退步了😣？

其实答案在你自己身体里，

四个4️⃣ 决定拔牙后疼痛程度的原因：

1) 拔牙时牙齿的状况
有活跃性感染的牙齿，拔完之后会比较痛
感染会改变周围组织的酸碱值，让麻药更难完全封住神经，发炎的组织在愈合过程中也会更敏感

2) 拔牙的难度
拔一颗已经松动的牙跟拔一颗牙根分叉或者在骨头里转弯的牙完全不一样
处理得越久，组织受到创伤的时间就越长

3) Dry socket（干槽症）
本来应该盖住拔牙洞的血块提早脱落了
骨头直接暴露在外，神经被空气和压力直接刺激
所以有时候第二天或第三天反而比第一天更痛

4) 你拔完之后做了什么（上次不痛，这次会不会就没那么认真跟着医生的指示做？）
用吸管喝东西、用力漱口、吃热的东西、抽烟
这些都会把血块冲掉，让已经开始愈合的地方重新暴露

🦷
